title: Identify Who Can Update Tote Exchange Settings And Note Project Finalization Context
summary: Use the training segment on Tote Exchange Settings to document the source-backed ownership context: the settings can be updated by the customer, but are generally finalized with the project team and UPS at the end of UAT. This runbook is for recording that distinction only and does not define permissions, approvals, or production change authority.
when_to_use: Use when documenting or answering questions about the source-stated ownership context for Tote Exchange Settings, especially when distinguishing who can update the settings versus how they are generally finalized during the project lifecycle.
not_for: Determining actual access control or permission models
Defining approval rules for production changes
Assigning escalation paths for configuration changes
Deciding who should make production changes from this source alone

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the training segment and record the ownership context exactly as stated in the source.

safety_notes: This is a documentation/reference procedure only.
Do not infer approval authority, access permissions, or operational change ownership beyond the source statement.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the training segment transcript or notes

access_required: Access to the training segment transcript or notes

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the Tote Exchange Settings page discussed in the ownership/finalization statement.

steps.title: Review the Tote Exchange Settings training segment
steps.purpose: Locate the source statement that describes update ownership and finalization context.
steps.instruction: Review the Tote Exchange Settings training segment and note the statement about who can update the settings.
steps.expected_result: The relevant source statement is identified in the training segment.
steps.healthy_condition: The segment reviewed is the Tote Exchange Settings discussion and includes the ownership/finalization statement.
steps.failure_condition: The reviewer cannot locate the relevant statement or is relying on a different segment.

actions.label: Review training segment
actions.exact_action: Review the Tote Exchange Settings training segment and note the statement about who can update the settings.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tote Exchange Settings interface and the segment associated with the statement about customer updates and UAT finalization.

stop_or_escalate_if: The source segment cannot be accessed or verified.
A stronger ownership or permission statement is required than what this source provides.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: These can be updated by the customer, but for the most part we kind of finalize it with the project team with UPS at the end of UAT.

steps.title: Record customer update capability
steps.purpose: Capture the source-backed statement about who can update the settings.
steps.instruction: Record that the source says these settings can be updated by the customer.
steps.expected_result: Documentation includes the statement that the customer can update the settings.
steps.healthy_condition: The note explicitly states update capability as 'updated by the customer.'
steps.failure_condition: The note omits the customer update statement or rewrites it into unsupported permission language.

actions.label: Document update capability
actions.exact_action: Record that the source says these settings can be updated by the customer.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Tote Exchange Settings page associated with the statement that these settings can be updated by the customer.

stop_or_escalate_if: Someone requests confirmation of actual access permissions or approval authority.
The documentation needs a production change owner not stated in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: These can be updated by the customer, but for the most part we kind of finalize it with the project team with UPS at the end of UAT.

steps.title: Record UAT finalization context
steps.purpose: Capture the source-backed statement about how the settings are generally finalized.
steps.instruction: Record that the source also says the settings are generally finalized with the project team and UPS at the end of UAT.
steps.expected_result: Documentation includes the statement that the settings are generally finalized with the project team and UPS at the end of UAT.
steps.healthy_condition: The note preserves the project team, UPS, and end-of-UAT context.
steps.failure_condition: The note changes 'generally finalized' into a mandatory approval rule or removes the UAT context.

actions.label: Document finalization context
actions.exact_action: Record that the source also says the settings are generally finalized with the project team and UPS at the end of UAT.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Tote Exchange Settings segment tied to the statement about finalization with the project team and UPS at the end of UAT.

stop_or_escalate_if: A formal approval workflow is needed.
A definitive production governance statement is required beyond the source wording.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: These can be updated by the customer, but for the most part we kind of finalize it with the project team with UPS at the end of UAT.

steps.title: Distinguish update capability from finalization context
steps.purpose: Ensure the documentation preserves the source's distinction without adding unsupported meaning.
steps.instruction: When documenting the configuration ownership context, distinguish between update capability and finalization context exactly as stated in the source.
steps.expected_result: The final note clearly separates customer update capability from general end-of-UAT finalization context.
steps.healthy_condition: The documentation reflects both parts of the statement without conflating them.
steps.failure_condition: The documentation treats update capability as equivalent to approval authority or finalization governance.

actions.label: Separate the two concepts
actions.exact_action: When documenting the configuration ownership context, distinguish between update capability and finalization context exactly as stated in the source.

stop_or_escalate_if: The distinction cannot be preserved without making unsupported assumptions.
Stakeholders require a formal ownership model not defined in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: These can be updated by the customer, but for the most part we kind of finalize it with the project team with UPS at the end of UAT.

steps.title: Avoid unsupported ownership or approval claims
steps.purpose: Prevent overstatement beyond what the source supports.
steps.instruction: Do not assign approval rules, access permissions, or escalation paths that are not explicitly provided by the source.
steps.expected_result: The documentation remains limited to the source-backed ownership context.
steps.healthy_condition: No unsupported approval, permission, or escalation details are added.
steps.failure_condition: The documentation introduces inferred access control, approval workflow, or escalation guidance not present in the source.

actions.label: Limit documentation to source facts
actions.exact_action: Do not assign approval rules, access permissions, or escalation paths that are not explicitly provided by the source.

stop_or_escalate_if: A stronger ownership, approval, or permission statement is needed because this source does not define access control details.
Someone asks who should make production changes based on this source alone.

success_criteria: A source-backed note states that Tote Exchange Settings can be updated by the customer.
The same note states that the settings are generally finalized with the project team and UPS at the end of UAT.
The documentation clearly distinguishes update capability from finalization context.
No unsupported approval, permission, or escalation details are added.
healthy_conditions: The documentation matches the source wording and intent.
The ownership context is recorded as a reference note rather than a permission model.
The UAT finalization context is preserved.
failure_conditions: The source statement cannot be verified from the referenced training segment.
The documentation infers access control or approval authority not stated in the source.
The documentation omits either the customer update statement or the end-of-UAT finalization context.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if a stronger ownership, approval, or permission statement is needed because this source does not define access control details.
Do not infer who should make production changes from this source alone.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tote Exchange Settings page and the associated training segment describing customer update capability and end-of-UAT finalization context.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: These can be updated by the customer, but for the most part we kind of finalize it with the project team with UPS at the end of UAT.

missing_details: The source does not define actual access permissions or access control mechanisms.
The source does not define approval rules for changing Tote Exchange Settings.
The source does not define escalation paths for configuration ownership disputes.
The source does not specify who should make production changes after UAT.
